name: Campo de Montiel Loop: La Solana to Villanueva de los Infantes
name_original: Villanueva de los Infantes desde La Solana – circular por el Castillo de Alhambra
description: This moderate 56.4-mile road cycling route from La Solana explores historical sites like Castillo de Alhambra and Villanueva de los Infantes
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Solana traverse a landscape characterized by open plains, agricultural areas, and rolling hills, with occasional volcanic features and historical sites. The region offers varied terrain suitable for road cyclists, from long, flat stretches to more challenging ascents. Routes often connect charming towns and villages, providing opportunities to experience the local culture. Best road cycling routes around La Solana The most popular road cycling route is Bolaños de Calatrava from Manzanares – circular via Moral de Calatrava and the Volcán La Mesnera, a 46.7 miles (75.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete. This moderate route features a mix of open roads and views of the Volcán La Mesnera. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de Peñarrolla y Ruidera desde Manzanares – circular por Argamasilla de Alba y Castillo de Alhambra, a difficult 75.6 miles (121.7 km) path. This route offers significant climbs and passes by historical castles and natural parks. Local road cyclists also love the Campo de Montiel Loop: La Solana to Villanueva de los Infantes, a 56.4 miles (90.8 km) trail leading through the open landscapes of Campo de Montiel, often completed in about 3 hours 50 minutes. Road cycling around La Solana is defined by open plains, gentle ascents, and routes connecting historical towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging road bike climbs. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 120 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Solana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around La Solana?
A: Road cycling routes around La Solana are characterized by open plains, agricultural areas, and rolling hills. You'll find varied terrain, from long, flat stretches ideal for steady rides to more challenging ascents, often with views of occasional volcanic features and historical sites. The region's network connects charming towns and villages, offering a diverse cycling experience.

Q: Are there beginner-friendly road cycling routes in La Solana?
A: Yes, La Solana offers routes suitable for beginners. For a moderate ride that provides a good introduction to the area's landscape, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086869491/bolanos-de-calatrava-from-manzanares-circular-via-moral-de-calatrava-and-the-volcan-la-mesnera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños de Calatrava from Manzanares – circular via Moral de Calatrava and the Volcán La Mesnera&lt;/a&gt;. This route covers 46.7 miles (75.2 km) with a mix of open roads and scenic views, making it accessible for those new to the region's cycling.

Q: Where can I find challenging road bike climbs near La Solana?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the region offers routes with significant climbs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086883816/castillo-de-penarrolla-y-ruidera-desde-manzanares-circular-por-argamasilla-de-alba-y-castillo-de-alhambra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñarrolla y Ruidera desde Manzanares – circular por Argamasilla de Alba y Castillo de Alhambra&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 75.6-mile (121.7 km) path that includes substantial elevation gains, passing by historical castles and natural parks.

Q: Are there any circular road bike routes available from La Solana?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as circular loops. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1080381304/campo-de-montiel-loop-la-solana-to-villanueva-de-los-infantes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campo de Montiel Loop: La Solana to Villanueva de los Infantes&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 56.4-mile (90.8 km) trail that takes you through the open landscapes of Campo de Montiel and returns to your starting point.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can see along the road cycling routes?
A: Road cycling routes around La Solana often pass by historical sites and charming towns. You might encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5475397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of La Solana and Church of Santa Catalina&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5516351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of the Santísimo Cristo del Valle&lt;/a&gt;. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086869491/bolanos-de-calatrava-from-manzanares-circular-via-moral-de-calatrava-and-the-volcan-la-mesnera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños de Calatrava from Manzanares&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Volcán La Mesnera.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in La Solana?
A: The region's climate generally allows for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures for longer rides, avoiding the peak heat of summer. These seasons also showcase the agricultural landscapes at their most vibrant.

Q: Are there road bike routes that pass through towns with coffee stops or places to rest?
A: Many routes around La Solana connect various towns and villages, providing ample opportunities for coffee stops and breaks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086894914/argamasilla-de-alba-from-la-solana-circular-by-manzanares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argamasilla de Alba from La Solana - circular by Manzanares&lt;/a&gt; route passes through Manzanares, a town where you can easily find places to refuel and rest.

Q: How long do road bike routes near La Solana typically take to complete?
A: The duration of road bike routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. Moderate routes, such as the 46.7-mile (75.2 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086869491/bolanos-de-calatrava-from-manzanares-circular-via-moral-de-calatrava-and-the-volcan-la-mesnera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bolaños de Calatrava from Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 3 hours. Longer, more challenging routes, like the 75.6-mile (121.7 km) &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086883816/castillo-de-penarrolla-y-ruidera-desde-manzanares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Peñarrolla y Ruidera desde Manzanares&lt;/a&gt;, may require over 5 hours.

Q: Are there any routes that explore natural parks or unique natural features?
A: Yes, some routes offer glimpses of the region's natural beauty. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086902115/villarrubia-de-los-ojos-y-daimiel-desde-manzanares-circular-por-tablas-de-daimiel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarrubia de los Ojos y Daimiel desde Manzanares – circular por Tablas de Daimiel&lt;/a&gt; route, for example, takes you near the Tablas de Daimiel National Park, known for its wetlands and birdlife, offering a different kind of scenery.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in La Solana?
A: Road cyclists often praise the varied terrain, which offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging climbs. The routes are appreciated for connecting historical towns and providing opportunities to experience local culture amidst open plains and gentle ascents.

Q: Are there any hidden gem road cycling routes in the La Solana area?
A: While many routes are well-known, exploring the smaller roads connecting the numerous villages can reveal quieter, less-trafficked paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086894914/argamasilla-de-alba-from-la-solana-circular-by-manzanares" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Argamasilla de Alba from La Solana - circular by Manzanares&lt;/a&gt; route offers a pleasant journey through the local countryside, providing a sense of discovery away from the busiest roads.
tips: The Plaza Mayor of Villanueva de los Infantes is considered one of the most beautiful main squares in Castilla-La Mancha, not in vain has it been declared a Historic-Artistic Site, with notable buildings such as the Town Hall, emblazoned mansions, porticoed gallery, the Hospital de Santiago, the Convent of Santo Domingo or the Church of San Andrés Apóstol.

It is located in La Carrasca, about 14 km north of Villahermosa. According to tradition, the Virgin Mary appeared to a shepherd named Juan Cortés on top of an oak tree, from which the name of the sanctuary comes. A hermitage was built on the site of the apparition, which over time became the current sanctuary.
Beautiful town that is located in what is called the Campo de Montiel, a large extension within the same bellow. The locality is crossed by the Río Cañamares and which goes west, towards Damiel.

You absolutely must visit this unique and striking castle. I recommend climbing to the top to enjoy the views from its privileged elevated position and imagining the times when such thick walls were necessary.
The town of La Solana is located to the southeast of the province of iudad Real, in a flat area with gentle hills, except for the heights of the Sierra de Alhambra located to the south.

faqs.question: What is the estimated cycling time and difficulty for this route?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate road cycling route, approximately 90.8 kilometers long with an elevation gain of 593 meters. It typically takes around 3 hours and 51 minutes to complete, requiring good fitness.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: While primarily a road cycling route, it's important to note that some segments may be unpaved and difficult to ride, which could be challenging for road bikes. Most of the route, however, is expected to have well-paved surfaces.

faqs.question: What historical sites and landmarks will I encounter on this ride?
faqs.answer: The route offers a rich blend of history and architecture. You'll pass by the impressive Alhambra Castle and explore the historic centers of Plaza Mayor de Villanueva de los Infantes and Plaza Mayor of La Solana. Other notable points include the Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Carrasca and the charming Carrizosa Village.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is designed as a circular loop, starting and ending in La Solana, passing through Villanueva de los Infantes and by the Castillo de Alhambra.

faqs.question: What are the parking options for cyclists in La Solana?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for cyclists are not detailed, La Solana, as a starting and ending point, typically offers public parking facilities within the town. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival for the most convenient spots.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Castilla–La Mancha region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be cool, so plan accordingly.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route that largely follows public roads. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs on the route itself, it's crucial to ensure your dog is safely secured in a bike trailer or basket and that you adhere to local leash laws and regulations, especially in towns and near historical sites.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route or the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle this route itself, as it primarily uses public roads. However, if you plan to visit attractions within the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Ruidera, it's recommended to check their official website for any specific regulations or fees that might apply to certain areas or activities within the park.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, it briefly intersects with sections of other paths such as the Sendero de los Chozos and the Circular Villanueva de los Infantes - Fuenllana.

faqs.question: Are there cafes or rest stops along the way?
faqs.answer: The route passes through several towns and villages, including Villanueva de los Infantes, Carrizosa, and La Solana. These settlements offer opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and rest.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling tour?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and length, it's recommended to bring sufficient water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, appropriate cycling attire for the weather, sun protection, and a fully charged phone. Consider carrying a map or GPS device, especially for the potentially unpaved sections.
